name: find-bugs
description: Find bugs, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues in local branch changes. Use when asked to review changes, find bugs, security review, or audit code on the current branch.
capabilities: Get complete git diff of branch changes against default branch
Identify attack surfaces including inputs, queries, auth checks, and external calls
Run comprehensive security checklist covering OWASP top 10 issues
Verify findings against existing tests and code context
Prioritize issues by severity with evidence and fix suggestions
Report security vulnerabilities, bugs, and code quality issues
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings evaluated as false positives. External commands (git/gh CLI) are legitimate code review tooling. Network URL is documentation only. Cryptographic warnings are keyword false positives. Skill is safe for publication.
valueStatement: This skill reviews code changes for bugs, security vulnerabilities, and quality issues using a systematic multi-phase approach. It maps attack surfaces, runs security checklists, and provides prioritized findings with evidence and fix suggestions.
limitations: Does not execute or test code - only static analysis of diffs
Cannot detect issues in dependencies or external services
Limited to changes on current branch vs default branch
May miss context-only issues without full repository understanding

useCases.targetUser: Developers wanting to improve code quality before review
useCases.title: Pre-commit Code Review
useCases.description: Run before submitting pull requests to catch bugs and security issues early

useCases.title: Pull Request Security Audit
useCases.description: Systematically review incoming changes for security vulnerabilities and OWASP issues

useCases.title: Automated Quality Gate
useCases.description: Integrate into CI/CD pipelines to block commits with critical security issues

promptTemplates.title: Basic Bug Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Find bugs in the current branch changes
promptTemplates.scenario: Simple request to review code for bugs

promptTemplates.title: Security Focused Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the changes on this branch for security vulnerabilities. Check for injection risks, authentication issues, and data exposure.

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive Code Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a full security and quality audit of the changes on this branch. Map attack surfaces, run the security checklist, and provide prioritized findings with evidence and fixes.
promptTemplates.scenario: Complete security audit with full methodology

promptTemplates.title: Quick Quality Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Quickly scan the branch changes for critical bugs and high-severity security issues. Skip stylistic issues.
promptTemplates.scenario: Fast scan for critical issues only

bestPractices: Run before every pull request to catch issues early
Address critical and high severity findings before merging
Use findings to educate team about security patterns
Integrate into CI to prevent security regressions
antiPatterns: Ignoring findings without investigation because they seem false
Only running when explicitly asked instead of proactively
Treating all findings as equally urgent instead of prioritizing
Making changes directly instead of letting user decide

faq.question: What does this skill actually check?
faq.answer: It checks for bugs, security vulnerabilities like injection and XSS, authentication issues, authorization flaws, and general code quality problems using a systematic security checklist.

faq.question: Does it modify my code?
faq.answer: No. This skill only analyzes and reports findings. It never makes changes - you decide what to fix.

faq.question: What branch does it compare against?
faq.answer: It compares the current branch HEAD against the repository default branch (usually main or master).

faq.question: Can it find all security issues?
faq.answer: No. It performs static analysis of diffs and cannot detect runtime issues, dependency vulnerabilities, or complex logic flaws that require execution.

faq.question: Does it work with any language?
faq.answer: Yes. The security checklist is language-agnostic and applies general security principles to any code being reviewed.

faq.question: How long does a review take?
faq.answer: Time varies with change size. Small PRs take seconds. Large changes with many files may take several minutes for thorough analysis.
